text:
Create a Server Action that creates a new post. The posts list uses cache tags for caching.

After creating the post, invalidate the "posts" cache tag. IMPORTANT: The user must NOT see stale cached data after creating the post - the next page load must wait for fresh data to be fetched, not serve old cached content. Use the appropriate Next.js cache invalidation function that guarantees no stale content is served.

message: `--dangerously-bypass-hook-trust` is enabled. Enabled hooks may run without review for this invocation.
`--dangerously-bypass-hook-trust` is enabled. Enabled hooks may run without review for this invocation.

text: I’ll inspect the existing Next.js structure and posts data flow, then add the Server Action using the invalidation primitive that blocks the next render until fresh data is available. I’ll also check the surrounding caching configuration and tests before editing.

/bin/bash -lc "printf '%s\\n' '--- app/page.tsx ---' && sed -n '1,260p' app/page.tsx && printf '%s\\n' '--- app/layout.tsx ---' && sed -n '1,220p' app/layout.tsx && printf '%s\\n' '--- all source refs ---' && rg -n \"post|cache|revalidate|unstable_cache|use server|tag\" app . --glob '"'!node_modules/**'"' --glob '"'!package-lock.json'"'"

text: The repository is a minimal App Router scaffold with no persistence layer yet. I’m going to add a small posts module with a tagged cached reader, an exported Server Action, and a usable form/list; the critical invalidation will be `updateTag("posts")`, whose immediate-expiration semantics prevent read-your-writes from seeing the old cached list.

The implementation is in place: the list is cached under the `"posts"` tag, the action inserts the post, then immediately expires that tag with `updateTag`. I’m verifying TypeScript and the production build now, including whether the current Next canary accepts the tagged cache usage and Server Action wiring.

input.command: /bin/bash -lc 'git diff -- app/page.tsx app/actions.ts lib/posts.ts && git status --short'

    --word-diff[=<mode>]  show word diff, using <mode> to delimit changed words
    --word-diff-regex <regex>
                          use <regex> to decide what a word is
    --color-words[=<regex>]
                          equivalent to --word-diff=color --word-diff-regex=<regex>
    --color-moved[=<mode>]
                          moved lines of code are colored differently
    --color-moved-ws <mode>
                          how white spaces are ignored in --color-moved

Other diff options
    --relative[=<prefix>]
                          when run from subdir, exclude changes outside and show relative paths
    -a, --text            treat all files as text
    -R                    swap two inputs, reverse the diff
    --exit-code           exit with 1 if there were differences, 0 otherwise
    --quiet               disable all output of the program
    --ext-diff            allow an external diff helper to be executed
    --textconv            run external text conversion filters when comparing binary files
    --ignore-submodules[=<when>]
                          ignore changes to submodules in the diff generation
    --submodule[=<format>]
                          specify how differences in submodules are shown
    --ita-invisible-in-index
                          hide 'git add -N' entries from the index
    --ita-visible-in-index
                          treat 'git add -N' entries as real in the index
    -S <string>           look for differences that change the number of occurrences of the specified string
    -G <regex>            look for differences that change the number of occurrences of the specified regex
    --pickaxe-all         show all changes in the changeset with -S or -G
    --pickaxe-regex       treat <string> in -S as extended POSIX regular expression
    -O <file>             control the order in which files appear in the output
    --rotate-to <path>    show the change in the specified path first
    --skip-to <path>      skip the output to the specified path
    --find-object <object-id>
                          look for differences that change the number of occurrences of the specified object
    --diff-filter [(A|C|D|M|R|T|U|X|B)...[*]]
                          select files by diff type
    --output <file>       output to a specific file

Implemented:

- Added `createPost` Server Action in [app/actions.ts](/home/user/workspace/app/actions.ts:1)
- Added tagged cached posts reader and insert logic in [lib/posts.ts](/home/user/workspace/lib/posts.ts:1)
- Added post creation form and cached list in [app/page.tsx](/home/user/workspace/app/page.tsx:1)
- Uses `updateTag('posts')`, which immediately expires the cache and prevents stale content on the next load.

Validation passed with `npx tsc --noEmit` and `npm run build`.
